title: SPARQL BIND does not always work on the latest Virtuoso release (07.20.3214)
description:
Consider the following two endpoints:

Endpoint 1: http://3cixty.eurecom.fr/sparql running Virtuoso 07.20.3214
Endpoint 2: http://91.250.81.138:8890/sparql running Virtuoso 07.20.3213

They have both the exact same dataset loaded, using the same method.
Consider the following simple SPARQL query:
SELECT DISTINCT *
WHERE {
    GRAPH <http://3cixty.com/milan/events> {?event a lode:Event .}
    ?event lode:atTime ?date.
    VALUES (?now) {("2015-8-21T00:00Z"^^xsd:dateTime)}
    ?date time:hasBeginning/time:inXSDDateTime ?datetimeBegin.
         BIND(xsd:dateTime(?datetimeBegin) - ?now AS ?dateDiffBegin)
    ?date time:hasEnd/time:inXSDDateTime ?datetimeEnd.
        BIND(xsd:dateTime(?datetimeEnd) - ?now AS ?dateDiffEnd)
}
LIMIT 100

The variables ?dateDiffBegin and ?dateDiffEnd are bound in the Endpoint 2 but NOT in the Endpoint 1. Is this a bug introduced in the newest version of Virtuoso?
I wonder if this bug is not also related to the issue #368 which is not closed yet.
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE events (event_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, event_name TEXT, event_date TEXT, location TEXT, attendees INTEGER CHECK (attendees >= 0));
INSERT INTO events (event_id, event_name, event_date, location, attendees) VALUES (1, 'Concert', '2023-10-01 20:00:00', 'Stadium', 100);
INSERT INTO events (event_id, event_name, event_date, location, attendees) VALUES (2, 'Conference', '2023-10-05 09:00:00', 'Convention Center', NULL);
SELECT event_name, event_date, attendees, CASE WHEN attendees IS NULL THEN 'No attendees' ELSE CAST(attendees AS TEXT) END AS attendee_status FROM events WHERE event_date > datetime('now');
UPDATE events SET attendees = COALESCE(attendees, 0) + 1 WHERE event_id = 1;
key_bug_features: BIND function fails to bind variables in latest Virtuoso version
